heading: Mandatory imposition of sanctions with respect to certain transactions with persons that evade sanctions imposed with respect to the Russian Federation
label: 8909 Mandatory imposition of sanctions with respect to certain transactions with persons that evade sanctions imposed with respect to the Russian Federation

children.text: materially violates, attempts to violate, conspires to violate, or causes a violation of any license, order, regulation, or prohibition contained in or issued pursuant to any covered Executive order, this chapter, or the Ukraine Freedom Support Act of 2014 ( et seq.); or22 U.S.C. 8921

children.label: A
children.text: any person subject to sanctions imposed by the United States with respect to the Russian Federation; or

children.label: B
children.text: any child, spouse, parent, or sibling of an individual described in subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Sanctions described
children.label: b Sanctions described
children.text: The sanctions described in this subsection are the exercise of all powers granted to the President by the International Emergency Economic Powers Act ( et seq.) to the extent necessary to block and prohibit all transactions in all property and interests in property of a person determined by the President to be subject to subsection (a) if such property and interests in property are in the United States, come within the United States, or are or come within the possession or control of a United States person.50 U.S.C. 1701

children.heading: Implementation
children.label: 1 Implementation
children.text: The President may exercise all authorities provided to the President under sections 203 and 205 of the International Emergency Economic Powers Act ( and 1704) to carry out subsection (b).50 U.S.C. 1702

children.heading: Penalties
children.label: 2 Penalties
children.text: A person that violates, attempts to violate, conspires to violate, or causes a violation of subsection (b) or any regulation, license, or order issued to carry out subsection (b) shall be subject to the penalties set forth in subsections (b) and (c) of section 206 of the International Emergency Economic Powers Act () to the same extent as a person that commits an unlawful act described in subsection (a) of that section.50 U.S.C. 1705

children.label: A
children.text: is in the vital national security interests of the United States; or

children.label: B
children.text: will further the enforcement of this chapter;

in the case of sanctions imposed under this section in connection with a covered Executive order described in subparagraph (A), (B), (C), or (D) of subsection (f)(1), a certification that the Government of the Russian Federation is taking steps to implement the Minsk Agreement to address the ongoing conflict in eastern Ukraine, signed in Minsk, Belarus, on , by the leaders of Ukraine, Russia, France, and Germany, the Minsk Protocol, which was agreed to on , and any successor agreements that are agreed to by the Government of Ukraine; andFebruary 11, 2015September 5, 2014

in the case of sanctions imposed under this section in connection with a covered Executive order described in subparagraphs (E) or (F) of subsection (f)(1), a certification that the Government of the Russian Federation has made significant efforts to reduce the number and intensity of cyber intrusions conducted by that Government.
a notice of and justification for the termination; and

children.label: A
children.text: the person is not engaging in the activity that was the basis for the sanctions or has taken significant verifiable steps toward stopping the activity; and

children.label: B
children.text: the President has received reliable assurances that the person will not knowingly engage in activity subject to sanctions under subsection (a) in the future.

children.label: A
children.text: Executive Order No. 13660 (79 Fed. Reg. 13493; relating to blocking property of certain persons contributing to the situation in Ukraine).

children.label: B
children.text: Executive Order No. 13661 (79 Fed. Reg. 15535; relating to blocking property of additional persons contributing to the situation in Ukraine).

children.label: C
children.text: Executive Order No. 13662 (79 Fed. Reg. 16169; relating to blocking property of additional persons contributing to the situation in Ukraine).

children.label: D
children.text: Executive Order No. 13685 (79 Fed. Reg. 77357; relating to blocking property of certain persons and prohibiting certain transactions with respect to the Crimea region of Ukraine).

children.label: E
children.text: Executive Order No. 13694 (80 Fed. Reg. 18077; relating to blocking the property of certain persons engaging in significant malicious cyber-enabled activities), relating to the Russian Federation.

children.label: F
children.text: Executive Order No. 13757 (82 Fed. Reg. 1; relating to taking additional steps to address the national emergency with respect to significant malicious cyber-enabled activities), relating to the Russian Federation.

children.heading: Foreign person
children.label: 2 Foreign person
children.text: The term “foreign person” has the meaning given such term in section 595.304 of title 31, Code of Federal Regulations (as in effect on ).August 2, 2017

children.heading: Structured
children.label: 3 Structured
children.text: The term “structured”, with respect to a transaction, has the meaning given the term “structure” in paragraph (xx) of section 1010.100 of title 31, Code of Federal Regulations (or any corresponding similar regulation or ruling).
